text:
Hey, I'm really sorry you're going through this. Breakups are genuinely one of the hardest things a person can experience, and whatever you're feeling right now — whether that's sadness, anger, relief, confusion, or all of it at once — that's completely okay. There's no wrong way to feel.

I know it probably doesn't help much to hear this right now, but what you're going through is temporary, even when it feels permanent. That hollow, aching feeling like something is missing from every single part of your day — that does get lighter with time. Not all at once, and not in a straight line, but it does.

Be gentle with yourself right now. You don't have to have it together. You don't have to figure out what comes next or what it all means. You're allowed to just sit with it for a while. Eat the comfort food, watch the old movies, cry when you need to cry. None of that is weakness — it's just being human.

And please don't isolate yourself too much. I know sometimes you just want to pull the covers over your head and disappear, and a little of that is fine, but let the people who love you show up for you. Let them bring you food or sit with you in silence or distract you with something completely ridiculous. You don't have to go through this alone.

One thing worth remembering is that the end of a relationship doesn't mean you failed. It means something ran its course. The love you put into it was real, and that matters, even if it didn't last forever.

You're going to be okay. I really believe that. And I'm right here.
